questions.question: What happens to undeclared props when you spread an object with v-bind onto a component?
options: Vue throws a warning listing the unknown props
They become fallthrough attributes and appear as HTML attributes on the root element
They are automatically added to the component's props definition

questions.question: Why does spreading all props cause unnecessary re-renders?
options: Vue cannot diff objects passed with v-bind
Every property in the spread is tracked as a prop, so any change triggers a re-render
Object spreading disables Vue's virtual DOM optimization
The spread operator creates a new object reference on every render

questions.question: In what scenario is v-bind="$attrs" the correct and intended pattern?
options: When passing a user object from an API to a display component
When building a thin wrapper component that intentionally forwards all attributes to an inner element
When you want to avoid declaring props for performance
When sharing state between sibling components
